title: Haroon

'''Haroon''' was the sole [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|penal]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Despayre]],<ref name="TFUCG" /> located in the [[Horuz system/Legends|Horuz system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]].<ref name="Atlas" /> The [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] surface of the moon<ref name="TFUCG" /> was exploited by the Galactic Empire after the relocation of their partially-constructed [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Despayre.<ref name="Atlas" /> A small [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation was set up on Haroon, where very large, specially cut crystals were extracted<ref name="TFUCG" /> for use in the construction of the Death Star. Near its completion in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Death Star [[Destruction of Despayre|destroyed]] Despayre in a three wave test fire of its [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], reducing the planet to a debris field, which presumably consumed Haroon in the process.<ref name="Atlas" /><ref name="X-wing">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ME0000797097_2.jpg|thumb|left|201x201px|Concept art by Greg Knight depicting an incomplete Death Star firing its superlaser at Haroon.]]
Originally, Haroon was only visually depicted as a satellite orbiting Despayre in a cutscene from the [[1993]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], which showed the destruction of Despayre by the Death Star's superlaser.<ref name="X-wing" /> Haroon was being fully developed by [[LucasArts]] for the video game ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'', initially as a completely original planet. It was to have been a green, crystal world where the Empire was mining the materials needed to create the immense focusing lenses on the Death Star. At least two pieces of concept art for Haroon were created by [[Greg Knight]], depicting a scene where an incomplete Death Star fires on the crystal-covered surface of the planet.<ref name="Art and Making of TFU">''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

The [[March]] [[2007]] issue of [[Wikipedia:Game Informer|''Game Informer'']] magazine first announced Haroon as a playable environment in ''The Force Unleashed'', and by this point Haroon was referred to as a moon.<ref name="Game Informer">[[Wikipedia:Game Informer|''Game Informer'']] #167</ref> The game's protagonist, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Galen Marek|secret apprentice]], was to journey there to rescue the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], who had been sentenced to a lifetime of labor in the mines of Haroon. However, issues arose when it was discovered that Ackbar's rescue from the Empire and recruitment into the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] were already depicted in other sources and did not fit with what the developers had in mind. Ultimately, Haroon was removed from the game and replaced with the more iconic [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] from [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="Art and Making of TFU" />

Nevertheless, Haroon eventually found its way into [[canon]] in some form. In [[2008]], ''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' first identified Haroon as Despayre's moon and attributed elements from ''The Force Unleashed''{{'s}} concept stages to it, such as its crystalline surface and the Imperial mining operation being conducted there.<ref name="TFUCG" /> In [[2009]], ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' also referenced Haroon and the moon was depicted in an image of Despayre's destruction, illustrated by [[Chris Trevas]].<ref name="Atlas" />

Though Haroon was described as being a green world in the concept stages of ''The Force Unleashed'',<ref name="Art and Making of TFU" /> ''X-wing'' depicts the moon as purple in [[Color/Legends|color]]<ref name="X-wing" /> and the illustration in ''The Essential Atlas'' shows the moon being illuminated by Despayre's destruction, making its intended hue unclear.<ref name="Atlas" /> While no source has yet to clarify whether Haroon was destroyed along with Despayre, the moon is no longer visible after Despayre is annihilated by the Death Star in the ''X-wing'' cutscene.<ref name="X-wing" />
